data.title: Why Microsoft Stock (MSFT) Is Under Pressure – and Why One Investor Says Concerns Are 'Addressable'
data.description:
Over the past few months, a shift in sentiment has been weighing on large-cap tech, and Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT) stock has been caught up in the hullabaloo, sliding about 23% year-to-date.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The issue: the massive AI capex spending.

Investors are concerned that the huge outlays of cash – Microsoft spent $37.5 billion last quarter – will pressure margins and deliver a disappointing ROI (Return on Investment).

On the flip side, Microsoft is already starting to see real returns on its earlier bets, with Azure and other cloud services posting 39% revenue growth last quarter. Moreover, the company ended the period with $625 billion in remaining performance obligations (RPO) for Microsoft Cloud, a clear sign that AI-driven demand is not just theoretical, but already locked into future revenue.

And yet, here too, commentators weren't fully at ease. Though the RPO represented 110% year-over-year growth, the duration for these potential sales also increased from 2 to 2.5 years in just one quarter.

One investor, known by the pseudonym Summit Research, understands the ROI and RPO worries. Nevertheless, the investor thinks a resolution for both is on the horizon.

"Both MSFT concerns now appear increasingly positioned for improvement as the agentic AI shift accelerates," states Summit Research.

Regarding the ROI issue, the investor notes that the company is focused on more than just Azure monetization, as it is working to actively balance capacity allocation through numerous revenue channels. This includes Microsoft's M365 Copilot and GitHub Copilot apps, and Summit Research argues that this approach is already paying off.

For instance, the company's M365 Copilot adoption hit 15 million paid licenses last quarter, up 160% year-over-year.

"The trend underscores how agentic functionality is deepening Microsoft's relevance across day-to-day enterprise workflows," adds Summit Research.

Regarding the increasing length of its RPO, the investor points out that Microsoft enjoyed a "multi-fold acceleration" in the last quarter (with a sequential jump from $392 to $625 billion). In addition, Azure customer commitments are increasing in scope, a dynamic that partly explains the growing RPO duration and should also lift Microsoft's growth prospects.

"Taken together, it's likely the agentic shift could help Microsoft address current market concerns," sums up Summit Research, who rates MSFT a Buy. (To watch Summit Research's track record, click here)

That's right where most of Wall Street is as well. With 33 Buys and 3 Holds, MSFT enjoys a Strong Buy consensus rating. Its 12-month average price target of $583.68 points to a 57% upside. (See MSFT stock forecast)
data.author: Gabe Ross

data.title: "Weigh on Customer Confidence": Ford Stock (NYSE:F) Slips, Dealers Get Concerned About Recalls
data.description:
Legacy automaker Ford (F) has seen a lot of recalls this year already. In fact, they recently saw another one. We will talk about that one later. For right now, there is a growing discontent among dealerships who are starting to get concerned about the growing number of recalls coming out of Ford, and what it may mean for future sales. Ford investors were no less concerned, as shares dipped modestly in Wednesday afternoon's trading.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The early reports so far do not look good for Ford. Over 7.3 million Ford vehicles have been recalled as of March 3, 2026. There have been more recalls staged between there and here, so we may be closer to eight million at this point. We must also consider that we are not even 90 days into the year yet, and Ford already accounts for roughly one in every three recalls staged in the entirety of 2026 so far.

This is leaving dealers biting their collective nails. They are the front line for these required fixes, as well as for future sales of the vehicles in question. And right now, there is growing concern that the sheer bulk of these recalls may prompt customers to reconsider a Ford purchase as being too much bother. Or worse, potentially, the site of their unexpected death.

Yes, Another Recall

This news came out roughly hours away from another recall, this time focused on 48,000 vehicles across several different models. Ford Ranger, Mustang, Maverick, Explorer, Escape, Bronco Sport and Bronco are included, as well as Lincoln Nautilus and Lincoln Corsair. The recall is posed thanks to the exhaust gas recirculation (EGR) valve, which can fail and create "…an unexpected loss of drive power."

If the valve fails while driving, that loss of drive power can increase the risk of a crash, reports note. Worse yet, Ford is working on developing a solution for this, which means there is no solution currently available, and your car may decide to fail at pretty much any time. Ford will notify impacted drives once the fix is ready, though. However, reports suggest that letters detailing the final remedy will not be sent out until September 2026.

Is Ford Stock a Good Buy Right Now?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on F stock based on three Buys, 11 Holds and one Sell assigned in the past three months, as indicated by the graphic below. After a 14.95% rally in its share price over the past year, the average F price target of $13.95 per share implies 18.98% upside potential.
data.author: Steve Anderson

data.title: Wall Street Warns of Elevated Recession Risks as Oil Surges
data.description:
Oil prices have surged 26% since the U.S.-Iran war began on February 28, pushing Wall Street to raise recession odds. Higher oil prices pose widespread problems for the economy and could bring inflation to 5% while dragging down gross domestic product (GDP) by 1% or more, according to EY-Parthenon Chief Economist Gregory Daco.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

"Against this backdrop, we now place the probability of recession at 40%, but stress that those odds could rapidly rise in the event of a more prolonged or severe Middle East conflict," wrote Daco in a note.

JPMorgan, Goldman Warn of Recession Risks

Earlier this week, JPMorgan lowered its 2026 year-end S&P 500 (SPX) price target to 7,200 from 7,500, warning that elevated oil and gas prices could depress consumer demand. Head of Global Markets Strategy Dubravko Lakos-Bujas added that oil spikes of 30% or greater have historically preceded recessions. 

In addition, Goldman Sachs raised its recession odds to 30% from 25%, citing the closure of the Strait of Hormuz and a higher unemployment rate projection of 4.6% by year-end. The firm also raised its December 2026 personal consumption expenditures (PCE) inflation forecast to 3.1% from 2.9%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Best Oil Stock ETFs as Oil Prices Keep Climbing 03/25/26
data.description:
Brent crude oil flowed upwards and downwards today as the future of the Iran war remained uncertain. Despite President Trump's assertion that talks between the U.S. and Iran had been "productive", Iran, at least, publicly, did not seem ready to lay down its drones and missiles just yet.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Brent crude was up 1.79% to $101.72 in late trading.

"The apparently less than warm Iranian response to US overtures, the countries' own seemingly incompatible demands and reports of fresh strikes on its Bushehr power plant are all helping to push oil prices back above the $100 per barrel mark – which really gets market alarm bells ringing," said Dan Coatsworth, head of markets at AJ Bell.

With oil prices still on the rise then, let's look at two oil and gas ETFs from our Best Oil and Gas ETFs list, which are likely to continue benefiting.

ProShares UltraShort Oil & Gas (DUG)

It is a strategically designed exchange-traded fund (ETF) that offers investors a unique opportunity to capitalize on the inverse performance of the oil and gas sector. Categorized under the energy sector, this ETF specifically targets the oil, gas, and consumable fuels niche, providing a powerful tool for those looking to hedge against or profit from declines in this volatile industry.

With its focus on shorting the oil and gas market, this ETF not only serves as a potential hedge against rising energy costs but also offers the possibility of significant returns during periods of negative sentiment or declining prices in the oil and gas markets.

It was up by 0.14% today, although is down 48% over the last three months.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: This Little-Known Company Is the Best Oil Stock to Buy Now, Says UBS Group (UBS)
data.description:
Swiss bank UBS Group (UBS) has named a little-known company as its top pick in the oil sector.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to UBS, Ovintiv (OVV) is the best oil stock for investors to buy now as the war in Iran continues and crude oil prices fluctuate wildly. You can be forgiven if you haven't heard of Ovintiv before. The company largely flies under the radar and isn't a household name like oil companies Chevron (CVX) or Shell (SHEL).

Based in Denver, Colorado, Ovintiv was known as Encana and based in Canada until 2020, when it relocated to the U.S. and underwent a rebrand. Today, Ovintiv is engaged in hydrocarbon exploration, with operations mostly in the Permian Basin of Texas.

Why OVV Stock?

So why is UBS bullish on OVV stock? The Swiss bank says the company has successfully addressed two concerns that previously weighed on its share price and valuation. The company's acquisition of NVA's Montney assets, combined with its recently announced Anadarko Basin divestiture, has largely resolved issues related to crude oil and condensate inventory as well as its high debt levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Microsoft (MSFT) Parts Ways With Its Diversity Chief
data.description:
Technology giant Microsoft (MSFT) says it is parting ways with its chief diversity officer Lindsay-Rae McIntyre amid a wave of human resources changes.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In a statement, Microsoft said that it is reorganizing its internal operations as it focuses more on growing its artificial intelligence (AI) technologies and products. McIntyre will leave the company at the end of March to become a chief people officer at another unnamed organization.

Microsoft is one of several companies to end or scale back its diversity programs since U.S. President Donald Trump returned to the White House. Several executives have left Microsoft in recent months, including gaming leader Phil Spencer and productivity software head Rajesh Jha.

Growing Pressure on Microsoft

The changes at Microsoft also come with software stocks under increasing pressure as concerns mount about competition from artificial intelligence (AI) models. MSFT stock is down 23% this year. In this environment, Microsoft is racing to show benefits and a return on the investments it is making in AI.

At the same time, Microsoft is hiring top talent to accomplish its AI goals. The company recently announced that its engineering teams will come together under corporate vice-president Mel Simpson. Microsoft is also hiring someone new to run its talent acquisition, with a focus on AI.

Is MSFT Stock a Buy?

Microsoft's stock has a consensus Strong Buy rating among 36 Wall Street analysts. That rating is based on 33 Buy and three Hold recommendations issued in the last three months. The average price target on MSFT stock of $586.41 implies 57% upside from current levels.
data.author: Joel Baglole

data.title: Trump Moves China Visit to May 15 for High-Stakes Summit with Xi
data.description:
White House Press Secretary Karoline Leavitt announced on Wednesday that the Trump-Xi summit has been rescheduled to May 14-15 in Beijing, China. Chinese President Xi Jinping, alongside First Lady Peng Liyuan, will travel to Washington, D.C. at a later date that has yet to be scheduled.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

The summit was originally planned for March 31 to April 2, although Trump said it would be delayed by "a month or so" in light of the U.S.-Iran war.

Rare Earths in Focus as U.S., China Prepare for Summit

Earlier this month, Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and Trade Representative Jamieson Greer met with Chinese Vice Premier He Lifeng and Vice Minister of Commerce Li Chenggang in Paris, with Bessent describing the discussions as "very good."

The upcoming summit will likely cover several key topics, including tariffs, Iran, Taiwan, and rare earths. Rare earths are crucial in the development of AI chips, smartphones, electric vehicles, and other technology, with China accounting for roughly 65% of global production.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Retail Traders Dump Single Stocks for First Time Since 2023
data.description:
Retail traders showed signs of capitulation on Monday, selling $20.6 million of shares. This marked the first day of net single-stock selling since November 2023, according to Vanda Research.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Trade QQQ with leverage
  • New trading tool for QQQ bulls/bears
  • Forget margin or options. Here's how the pros trade QQQ

Retail demand has edged lower since the start of the U.S.-Iran war on February 28, with the S&P 500 (SPX) falling by 4% since then. "The trend since the start of March has been one of gradually receding retail participation, alongside systematic deleveraging and only modest buying from long-only and hedge fund investors on the other side," said Vanda Macro Strategist Ruta Prieskienyte.

Retail Traders Pick Up $262 Million of Shares after Brief Selling Spree

Retail traders have emerged as reliable buyers of downside price action since the bull market began three years ago. A break in the trend could create issues for the benchmark index as traders assess the impact of the U.S.-Iran war.

However, the selling didn't last long, as retail traders purchased $262.3 million worth of shares on Tuesday from the market open to 12:50 p.m. Eastern Time. The group purchased a record amount of shares last year, exceeding the previous record set in 2021 by 17%.
data.author: Eddie Pan

data.title: Regional Bank ETFs Under Pressure as Ohio CD Rates Hit 4% and Investors Flee to Gold
data.description:
Ohio certificate of deposit (CD) rates, which are fixed-interest savings products offered by banks, have climbed above 4% APY. As a result, one of the state's regional banks, LCNB Corporation (LCNB), is struggling to retain deposits despite offering higher rates.

Claim 30% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

This same pressure is now extending to regional bank ETFs, including the iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF (IAT) and the SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF (KRE). Rising funding costs and geopolitical tensions appear to have rattled investors, prompting them to flee to safe-haven assets like gold.

Gold Gains as Regional Bank ETFs Face Pressure

Rising CD rates force banks to pay higher interest to keep deposits, increasing their funding costs and reducing profits. Because regional bank ETFs like IAT and KRE hold shares of these banks, weaker earnings can drag down their performance and prompt investors to pull back.

Geopolitical tensions in early March added to pressure on regional bank ETFs, with KRE and IAT down 5.80% and 6.80%, respectively, over the past month. This follows the U.S.-Israeli strike on Iran in late February, which triggered an oil price spike and renewed inflation fears. Investors grew more wary of prolonged high interest rates, deepening their concerns about regional bank stability, which in turn weighed on both KRE and IAT ETFs.

Historical stress in regional bank ETFs has often driven investors toward safe-haven assets. Gold hit an all-time high in late January 2026 as U.S.-China trade tensions and ensuing geopolitical developments bolstered investor demand. Meanwhile, central banks have been buying gold at a record pace for several years, and JPMorgan (JPM) projects continued strong demand in 2026, adding pressure on U.S. regional bank ETFs as investors seek safer assets.

Top Holdings Boost Regional Bank ETFs' Outlook

Despite pressure on regional bank ETFs, several individual holdings within the SPDR S&P Regional Banking ETF and the iShares U.S. Regional Banks ETF have shown strong upside potential, according to TipRanks forecast data.

For KRE:

  • Banc of California Inc. (BANC): Analysts rate this stock a Strong Buy, with a projected average price target of $24.40 and an implied upside of 39.5%
  • United Community Banks Inc. (UCB): Analysts rate this stock a Moderate Buy, projecting an average price target of $115.39, with an implied upside of 269.5%
  • Coastal Financial Corp. (CCB): Analysts also rate this stock a Moderate Buy, with an average price target of $135.00 and an implied upside of 75.3%.
data.author: Joy Iyke
